## [15:05 - 18:07]

a problem and you're solving it. Define a problem then solve it and move through it this way. Okay so there's one more thing I want to layer in here that really makes this whole strategy pop and that's the mental triggers. So this is the deal. There are certain triggers that create enormous influence in us, in our brains that cause us to make decisions that really cause us to take actions. And these mental triggers they exist in every one of us and they have been with us as humans for thousands of years and it's very likely they'll be with us for thousands of years. So no matter who you are, where you live in the world, these mental triggers definitely have an impact on you and your decision making and me and my decision making. We're all susceptible to these triggers. So let me just go through a few of them. I've actually got 20 of these that I have identified but I don't have time to go through all 20 in this video. So let's just go through a few of the top ones. So first of all is community. Community is a very powerful trigger and we will, it's like if we perceive ourselves as being part of a community, we will act in accordance to how we think the people in that community are supposed to act. Really powerful. Now the one is anticipation. And this is the idea almost like where we anticipate something happening, anticipating events. Think about how powerful events are, you know, like birthdays or graduations or holidays, how we look forward to those. Think about the way TV is, especially like almost any TV shows, they leave these, they have these cliffhangers at the end of the show. They leave you anticipating and pull you in for the next show. Even the most basic sitcoms now use anticipation. Then there's authority. Authority is incredibly powerful trigger. It's a great one to use in your launches and it's a great, I mean the launch process really brings out the authority. It's one of the most powerful. And that's basically when we perceive someone as being an authority, we listen to them. They are very influential. I mean almost any type of uniform, like a police officer, a doctor, whatever, that creates authority. It's very, very powerful. Reciprocity is another great trigger. And that's the idea of if we give something to someone, they're going to feel an obligation to give something back to us. And it works the other way around. So if you give a gift to someone, they're going to feel like they want to give a gift back to you. Or if someone gives you a gift, you're going to want to feel like you need to give them a gift back. Then there's social proof. And this is the idea where if we see a lot of other people doing something, then we're going to think, well, maybe there's something there and we're going to be more inclined to do that as well. And then finally, there's scarcity. Scarcity is just like if there is less of something, then we want it more. I mean, if it's gold, diamonds, ferraris, whatever, just the fact that there's fewer of something instantly raises that value and gets us more interested. So I mean, those triggers, like I said, they're everywhere in our lives. They work everywhere in their lives. But I want to talk about how you can take these and layer them into your launch.
